<!-- Generated by corgiterm-terminal-rebuild-plan workflow (wf_734fe19f-47a), 2026-06-02. -->

Both checks confirm the inputs. Notable: `fc-list` returned nothing and `/System/Library/Fonts/` showed no Menlo/Monaco matches in that grep (the brief states Menlo/Monaco are present via Core Text, not fontconfig). This is itself load-bearing for Phase 0 and the verification harness, so I carry it forward as a caveat: on macOS, GTK/Pango font resolution goes through Core Text, not fontconfig, so `fc-list` is not the right probe. I confirmed `screencapture` exists, rust is 1.94 nightly (>= alacritty MSRV 1.85), and the workspace pins `alacritty_terminal = "0.24"` (phantom dep) and `vte = "0.13"`.

Here is the decision document.

---

# CorgiTerm Terminal Core Rebuild: Decision Document

Author: Lead architect synthesis. Date: 2026-06-02. Status: decision-ready, pending the user confirmations in section 7.

All file:line citations were read or grepped during verification. Engine-API method names from the evaluations that were marked unverified (not vendored in this repo) are carried forward as directional, not exact, and are flagged inline.

---

## 1. Root cause (verified)

The garble is not one bug. It is a structural mismatch between a per-char `String`-content cell grid that advances the cursor by exactly one column per char, and a renderer that draws each glyph at its own natural Pango advance while stepping columns by a single averaged-and-ceiled cell width. The missing default font makes it worse by forcing a proportional fallback whose "average char width" is meaningless. Three causes are independently CONFIRMED by reading the code; four more are contributing factors at medium/low confidence.

Confirmed primary causes:

- **(A) Glyph natural-advance vs fixed averaged grid pitch.** One `cell_w = (metrics.approximate_char_width() / pango::SCALE).ceil()` is used for every cell (`terminal_view.rs:301`), cells are placed at `x = padding + col_idx*cell_w` (`:507`), but each glyph is drawn with `layout.set_text(&cell.content)` + `pangocairo::functions::show_layout` (`:387-390`) with no `set_width` / `letter_spacing` / monospace constraint (grep over the file found zero of those). Natural advance != cell_w produces overlap (advance > cell_w) or gaps (advance < cell_w).
- **(B) Hardcoded missing default font, no monospace enforcement.** Default `font_family: "Source Code Pro"` at `corgiterm-config/src/lib.rs:158` and the renderer fallback literal `("Source Code Pro".to_string(), 11.0)` at `terminal_view.rs:294` (both confirmed by grep just now). That font is not installed on this Mac, so Pango falls back to a possibly-proportional face; `approximate_char_width` on it (`:301`) is a meaningless average, directly feeding cause A. There is no check that the resolved face is fixed-pitch.
- **(C) No wide-char width model.** `terminal.rs:227` unconditionally does `cursor.1 += 1` after writing one cell; `Cell.content` is a `String` (`terminal.rs:67`) with no width field, and grep over `terminal.rs` for `width`/`unicode_width`/`wcwidth`/`is_wide`/`grapheme` returned zero. A 2-column CJK/emoji glyph occupies one logical cell but renders ~2 cell widths, overrunning `col+1` (renderer treats every column as one `cell_w`, `:507`).

Contributing causes carried forward (lower confidence, fix opportunistically):

- Baseline math centers the ascent box instead of placing a true text baseline: `text_y = y + (cell_h - ascent)/2.0` (`terminal_view.rs:388`), reused for the cursor glyph; causes vertical drift/clipping with descenders or a tall fallback line-height (medium).
- Per-cell Pango layout reshaping every frame for the whole grid, no glyph cache, no damage gate on `draw_func` (`:502-570`); can drop frames and leave stale glyphs under fast output (low).
- Combining marks / ZWJ clusters split across cells (each `print()` is one char into its own cell, `:223,:227`); base+accent or ZWJ emoji render separated (medium).
- Integer `.ceil()` of `cell_w` causes mild rightward drift even with a correct monospace font (medium); a softer, persistent version of A.
- Tab handling (`:374-377`) and eager last-column wrap with no pending-wrap flag (`:216-228`) are correctness gaps that misalign specific programs (low).

The decisive point: causes A and C cannot be fixed inside the current `String`-content, +1-advance cell model. They require an engine that tracks per-cell width and guarantees the wide-char spacer invariant. Cause B is a one-line config fix independent of the engine.

---

## 2. Engine recommendation

Ranked from the supplied scores (1-5, higher better; "effort" and "risk" already given as inverse so higher = less effort / less risk):

| Engine | Correctness | macOS fit | GTK4 integ | Effort (inv) | Risk (inv) | Maint | Verdict |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| **alacritty_terminal 0.26** | 5 | 5 | 4 | 3 | 4 | 5 | **PICK (#1)** |
| wezterm-term + termwiz | 5 | 5 | 3 | 3 | 2 | 2 | #3 (supply-chain blocked) |
| libghostty-vt | 5 | 3 | 3 | 1 | 2 | 4 | Defer (#4 now, strong later) |
| vte4 GNOME widget | 5 | 2 | 5 | 4 | 2 | 5 | Honest baseline, rejected on macOS risk + control |

### #1 pick: alacritty_terminal 0.26.0 (model), keep the existing pango/cairo GTK4 DrawingArea renderer.

Rationale specific to this app:

- It is a like-for-like swap, not a rearchitecture. The current core already runs a vte-0.13 `Perform` state machine (`terminal.rs:527` `process()` -> `parser.advance(&mut state, byte)`). alacritty does the same shape internally (`Processor::advance` over its own vte). The blast radius for emulator internals is essentially one file (`terminal_view.rs`), because every other consumer (split_pane, tab_bar, window, ai_panel, history_search, recording_panel, broadcast) talks only to `TerminalView`'s high-level methods, never the grid.
- It directly fixes the two unfixable-in-place causes: wide-char column accounting via the grid's `WIDE_CHAR` / `WIDE_CHAR_SPACER` flags (cause C), and it removes CorgiTerm's hand-rolled `resize`/reflow (`terminal.rs:549-680`), itself a likely garble source. Cause B is fixed by the Phase 0 font change regardless.
- It is published, semver-stable, Apache-2.0, MSRV 1.85 (local toolchain verified 1.94.0-nightly), first-class on aarch64-apple-darwin, and is already declared in `Cargo.toml:64` as a phantom dep (currently used by nothing). Bump the pin to 0.26.
- It exposes exactly the readout surface the renderer needs: a renderable-content iterator with per-cell text + fg/bg + flags, cursor, selection, display offset for scrollback, plus a selection-to-string path that also serves the AI-panel "read screen text" need, and native OSC-8 hyperlinks. (Method names `renderable_content`, `selection_to_string`, `Term::resize`, `Term::damage`, `Cell::flags`, `Processor::advance` were verified against docs.rs 0.26.0 by the evaluator but are NOT vendored here; treat as directional and confirm against `cargo doc` once the dep is added.)

Two honest caveats that gate effort (this is why effort scored 3, not 5):

1. **No Sixel/Kitty graphics in the engine.** alacritty drops graphics escape sequences. CorgiTerm's `corgiterm-core/src/images.rs` Kitty/Sixel parsers exist but are currently dead (zero consumers, verified). If image rendering is ever wanted, it must be a sidecar: sniff APC/DCS sequences around `advance`, decode with `images.rs`, composite RGBA in `draw_func`. For parity today this is a no-op (the feature is not wired now).
2. **The model swap removes overlap/misalignment, it does not deliver GPU-class crispness.** Glyphs still rasterize through Pango+Cairo+CoreText on macOS. That is acceptable and correct, but do not promise Alacritty-level sharpness from a model-only swap; a GPU GLArea/glyphon renderer is a separate later project.

### When #2 would be the better call instead

- **Choose wezterm-term + termwiz** only if Sixel/iTerm2 images and rich OSC-8 hyperlinks are must-have on day one AND the team accepts git-pinning to an exact unpublished rev plus vendoring, and accepts never publishing CorgiTerm to crates.io. wezterm-term is the most complete model (native wide-char, scrollback, selection, images), but the model crate is unpublished (publish issue #6663 open and unanswered since Feb 2025); the only crates.io route is the stale third-party `tattoy-wezterm-term 0.1.0-fork.5` (Jul 2025). Capability 5/5, supply chain disqualifying for a stable dependency. This is the fallback if alacritty's missing-images limitation becomes a hard requirement.
- **Choose libghostty-vt** later, not now. The user's premise of "GPU Metal renderer from the library" is false: the Metal renderer lives in the Ghostty app, not the library; libghostty-vt is a VT-state engine only, so you still write the pango/cairo path either way. It also adds a Zig 0.15 build gate (Zig verified absent on this box), an untagged alpha FFI (`libghostty-rs` has zero releases), and three must-preserve features (hyperlinks, clipboard, sixel) not yet in its API. Re-evaluate once it ships a tagged `libghostty-vt` with those APIs and a macOS static-link precedent exists.
- **vte4 GNOME widget** honestly scores best on correctness+effort+maintenance and, correcting the original brief, IS installable on this Mac via `brew install vte3` (the formula builds the GTK4 variant; verified by the evaluator against the Homebrew formula, not installed here). One `feed_async` call plus `get_text_range` would erase all three garble causes for free. It is rejected anyway for two concrete reasons, stated plainly: (1) macOS is VTE's tier-2 target with documented GTK4-on-Quartz renderer glitches (GNOME/gtk #4165) and no verified shipping GTK4-VTE-on-macOS precedent (macOS fit 2, risk 2); (2) adopting the widget forfeits the deep cell-rendering / AI-integration control the user explicitly wants, and the user already rejected the VTE widget by name. If macOS-primary were dropped and Linux-first were acceptable, vte4 would be the rational pick. It is not, so it is the baseline any alacritty rebuild must beat, not the choice.

Decision: adopt alacritty_terminal 0.26.0 as the model; keep GTK4/libadwaita chrome and portable-pty unchanged; render with the existing grid-snapped Pango/Cairo DrawingArea, corrected per section 4.

---

## 3. Phased migration plan

Every phase is behind a feature boundary so it is independently revertible. Effort estimates assume one engineer familiar with the codebase; they are rough.

### Phase 0: immediate cheap mitigation (PARTIAL RELIEF, NOT THE FIX)

- **Files:** `crates/corgiterm-config/src/lib.rs:158`, `crates/corgiterm-ui/src/terminal_view.rs:294`.
- **Do:** Change both hardcoded `"Source Code Pro"` defaults to the Pango generic family `"monospace"` (Pango maps this through Core Text to an installed fixed-pitch face on macOS and to a fixed-pitch face on Linux). Do NOT hardcode `"Menlo"`: it is macOS-only, and the app also targets Linux. Generic `"monospace"` is the portable correct default. Keep the user's configured font honored when set; only the fallback literal changes.
- **Why labeled partial:** This addresses cause B only. It makes `approximate_char_width` meaningful again (a real monospace advance), which reduces A's symptom, but A's per-glyph-advance-vs-fixed-pitch mismatch and C's wide-char overrun both remain. Box-drawing and CJK/emoji will still garble. Be explicit with users: Phase 0 is "stop the worst proportional-fallback garble," not "fixed."
- **Caveat carried forward (verification just now):** `fc-list` returned nothing on this box, which is expected: GTK on macOS resolves fonts via Core Text, not fontconfig. So do not validate the font fix with `fc-list`; validate it by running the app (Phase 4) and by reading the resolved face name from a Pango `FontDescription` debug log.
- **Effort:** under 1 hour. **Rollback:** revert two string literals (`git checkout` the two files).

### Phase 1: introduce the engine behind a Rust trait boundary

- **Files:** new `crates/corgiterm-core/src/engine.rs` (trait + alacritty-backed impl); `crates/corgiterm-core/Cargo.toml` (add `alacritty_terminal`); workspace `Cargo.toml:64` (bump pin `0.24` -> `0.26`); a build flag in `corgiterm-config` or a cargo feature `engine-alacritty`.
- **Do:** Define a `TerminalEngine` trait that captures the minimal contract from the consumer map: `feed(&mut self, &[u8])`; `resize(&mut self, rows, cols)`; `viewport_cells(offset) -> impl Iterator<Item=RenderCell>` where `RenderCell { text, fg, bg, flags, width, col, row }`; `cursor() -> CursorState { row, col, visible, shape }`; `scrollback_len() -> usize`; `row_text(row) -> String`; `selection_to_string() -> Option<String>`; `set_max_scrollback(usize)`. Provide two impls: the existing `corgiterm-core::Terminal` (adapter) and a new `AlacrittyEngine` wrapping `Arc<FairMutex<Term<EventProxy>>>` + `Processor`. `EventProxy` implements alacritty's `EventListener` and forwards `PtyWrite`, `Title`, `Bell`, `ClipboardStore`, `Wakeup` onto the existing crossbeam channel (`TerminalEvent`). Critically, wire `PtyWrite` back to `Pty::write` so DSR / bracketed-paste replies reach the shell (interactive apps break otherwise). `TerminalView` selects the impl by feature flag; default stays the old engine until Phase 2 passes verification.
- **Threading note (load-bearing):** today `Term` is `RefCell`-borrowed on the GTK draw thread (`terminal_view.rs:276`). alacritty's idiomatic use moves the parser to the reader thread behind `FairMutex<Term>`. GTK is strictly main-thread-only on macOS, so every Term->UI event must marshal through `glib::timeout_add_local` (the existing AI-panel async pattern is the template). Get this right before claiming parity.
- **Effort:** 1.5-2.5 days. **Rollback:** the engine is behind a cargo feature; ship with old engine default. `git revert` the trait commit or flip the flag. No consumer code changed yet.

### Phase 2: renderer rewrite to the correctness spec (section 4)

- **Files:** `crates/corgiterm-ui/src/terminal_view.rs` (the draw_func at `:273` and helpers `:301`, `:387-412`, `:502-570`, `:587-635`).
- **Do:** Replace the grid/scrollback/cursor reads with `engine.viewport_cells(offset)` and `engine.cursor()`; delete the manual `effective_offset` scrollback-index math (`~:419-489`). Compute a fixed `cell_w`/`cell_h` from a true monospace advance (not `approximate_char_width().ceil()`), draw per-cell snapped to `col*cell_w`, honor `width==2` cells by spanning two columns and skipping the spacer cell, and place glyphs on a real baseline. Implement the engine's `fg`/`bg` color enum -> RGBA resolver against the existing theme palette (`load_theme_colors`, `terminal_view.rs:92-126`). Handle the default-bg sentinel coupling (`:345`) by switching to a `has_explicit_bg` flag rather than comparing to the literal `[30,27,22]`.
- **Effort:** 2-4 days (this is the long pole and where the original bug lived). **Rollback:** Phase 1's feature flag still selects the old engine + old draw path; keep the old `draw_func` behind the flag until Phase 4 signs off, then delete.

### Phase 3: re-wire each preserved feature (consumer map)

- **Files:** `terminal_view.rs` (selection `:1587-1675`, URL detect `:425-447`/`:1105-1131`, hints `:899-920`, search `:1462-1518`, `get_visible_lines` `:1852`, bell `:1722-1740`, copy `:928-976`), `split_pane.rs`, `tab_bar.rs`, `session.rs:158,182`.
- **Do:** Back `get_visible_lines`, copy, URL detect, hints, and search with `engine.row_text(row)` (keep the public method signatures so downstream is untouched). Migrate selection to engine `(row,col)` Point/Selection or keep UI cell-coords if the engine guarantees stable viewport mapping. **Fix two pieces of existing theater while here:** (a) wire engine `Bell` -> `trigger_visual_bell()` (today `event_receiver()` is never read, so the visual bell never fires); (b) optionally restore OSC title -> tab title, or keep the cwd-polling titler. Update `session.rs` `Session.terminal` field type, or drop its unused live-terminal fields (the live path bypasses `Session` entirely). Replace Linux-only `xdg-open` (`:1129`) with `open` on macOS (pre-existing bug).
- **Effort:** 2-3 days. **Rollback:** each feature rewire is a separate small commit; revert individually.

### Phase 4: verification (section 6) and old-path deletion

- **Do:** Run the torture test and headless grid tests; capture macOS screenshots; only after green, delete the old `corgiterm-core::Terminal` engine path, the dead `corgiterm-terminal` GPU crate, and unused `wgpu`/`glyphon` deps (section 7 confirmation gate). Fix the false "VTE Terminal Widget" About text (`dialogs.rs:205,252`).
- **Effort:** 1-2 days. **Rollback:** deletion is the last step; until it lands everything is dual-path and revertible by feature flag.

Total rough effort: 8-13 days. Reversibility is preserved end-to-end by the Phase 1 trait + cargo feature; nothing is irreversible until Phase 4's explicit deletion gate.

---

## 4. Renderer-correctness spec (engine-independent contract)

The new draw path MUST meet all of the following, regardless of which engine wins:

1. **Fixed grid snapping.** Every cell is placed at integer `x = padding + col*cell_w`, `y = padding + row*cell_h`. `cell_w`/`cell_h` are computed ONCE from a resolved monospace face's true advance and line height, not from `approximate_char_width().ceil()`. Use the face's reported fixed advance (or measure a representative glyph like "M"/"0"); do not average across glyphs.
2. **Per-run shaping, not per-cell, OR a GPU glyph atlas.** Do not call `set_text` + `show_layout` once per cell (current `:387-390`). Batch contiguous same-style cells into a single Pango run and let HarfBuzz shape the run, then position the run on the grid; or, in the future GPU path, rasterize each glyph once into an atlas and blit per cell. v1 target: per-run Pango shaping with grid snapping. This also kills the per-frame full-grid reshape performance smell.
3. **Wide-char 2-column handling.** Honor a per-cell width. `width==2` cells draw their glyph spanning two `cell_w` columns; the immediately following spacer cell is skipped (advance x by `cell_w`, draw nothing). Combining marks render onto the base cell, not a new cell. The pixel<->cell hit-test math (used by selection, URL, hints, search, click) must account for double-width cells so clicks land on the right logical cell.
4. **Robust monospace font resolution + fallback chain.** Resolution order: user-configured family (if set and resolvable) -> generic `"monospace"` (portable, Core Text on macOS / fontconfig on Linux). Log the actually-resolved face name at startup. Assert/measure that the resolved face is fixed-pitch; if not, fall back to generic monospace. Never ship a hardcoded family that may be absent (the original bug). Define an explicit fallback for missing glyphs (Pango handles per-glyph fallback; ensure the fallback face is also monospace-metrics-compatible or the run is re-measured).
5. **Cursor / selection / underline overlays.** Cursor drawn as one of the four existing shapes (block/underline/bar/hollow) at the engine-reported `(row,col)`, honoring a visibility flag; under a block cursor, invert the glyph beneath it. Selection highlight and search/URL/hint highlights are drawn as background rects under the affected cells using the same grid snapping. These overlays must use the identical `cell_w`/`cell_h` and wide-char accounting as the glyph pass so they never drift from the text.
6. **Baseline math.** Position each glyph run on a true text baseline: `baseline_y = row*cell_h + ascent` (with `cell_h = ceil(ascent + descent + line_gap)`), then draw the Pango layout so its baseline sits at `baseline_y`. Do not center the ascent box (current `:388` `(cell_h - ascent)/2.0` is wrong and reused for the cursor at `:609`). Descenders and tall fallback line-heights must not clip into adjacent rows.
7. **Default-bg sentinel.** Do not special-case a literal RGBA triple to decide whether to paint a cell background (current `:345` hardcodes `[30,27,22]`). The engine adapter must expose a `has_explicit_bg` boolean per cell; only explicit backgrounds are painted, default-bg cells inherit the widget background.
8. **Damage-gated redraw (correctness + perf).** Drive `queue_draw_area` from the engine's damage region rather than repainting the whole grid every 16ms timer tick. Stale-frame garble under fast output is a contributing cause; damage tracking removes it.

---

## 5. Feature-preservation matrix

Chosen engine = alacritty_terminal 0.26. Engine API names marked (unverified) are from the evaluator's docs.rs reading, not vendored here; confirm against `cargo doc -p alacritty_terminal` after adding the dep.

| Feature | Current API (file:line) | Replacement in alacritty_terminal | Risk |
|---|---|---|---|
| Screen render (read grid) | `Terminal::grid() -> &[Vec<Cell>]` (`terminal.rs:709`); `Cell{content,fg,bg,attrs}` (`terminal.rs:64-98`) | `renderable_content().display_iter` yields `Indexed<Cell>` with `c`, `fg`, `bg`, `flags` (unverified names) | Low. 1:1 map behind the trait adapter. |
| Scrollback + scroll-into-history | `scrollback() -> &[Vec<Cell>]` (`:714`); `set_max_scrollback` (`:719`) | grid history + `display_offset`; `Scroll::Delta/PageUp`; `history_size()` (unverified) | Low. Deletes the manual offset math (`~:419-489`). |
| Cursor position + render | `cursor() -> (usize,usize)` (`:699`) | `renderable_content().cursor` (point + shape) (unverified) | Low. Blink stays UI-side (`:1378`). |
| Colors + SGR attrs | `Cell.fg/bg:[u8;4]` + `CellAttributes`; `apply_sgr` (`:251-337`) | engine `Color` enum (Named/Spec/Indexed) + `Flags` bitfield; write a Color->RGBA resolver against theme | Medium. Default-bg sentinel (`:345`) must move to `has_explicit_bg`. |
| Feed PTY bytes + recovery | `safe_process(&[u8]) -> TerminalHealth` (`:732`); `TerminalHealth` (`:9-19`) | `Processor::advance(&mut term, &bytes)` (unverified); health/reset surface dropped (engine parses invalid seqs gracefully) | Low. The 7 health methods have one logging consumer only. |
| Resize / reflow | `resize(TerminalSize)` hand-rolled (`:549-680`) | `Term::resize(TermSize)` native reflow (unverified) | Medium. Removes a likely garble source; PtySize-vs-grid clipping hack (`:498-506`) re-evaluated. |
| PTY spawn/read/write/resize | `Pty::spawn/write/reader_clone/resize` (`pty.rs:75,168,246,155`) | UNCHANGED (portable-pty, engine-independent) | None. Keep as-is. |
| Copy / select-all text | `grid()`+`scrollback()` per-cell concat (`:928-976`) | `selection_to_string()` (unverified) or `row_text` join | Low. Fixes wide-char/empty-cell copy bugs. |
| Text selection (drag) | UI-only `Selection{start,end:(row,col)}` (`:50-58`,`:1906`) | engine `Selection`/`Point` + `viewport_to_point` (unverified), or keep UI coords if viewport map is stable | Medium. Needs stable viewport mapping or migrate to engine Point. |
| URL detect + Ctrl+click | UI regex over `grid()` lines (`:425-447`,`:1105-1131`) | per-row text via `row_text`; OPTIONAL native OSC-8 `Cell.hyperlink()` (unverified) | Low. `xdg-open` (`:1129`) -> `open` on macOS (pre-existing bug). |
| Hint mode (foot-style) | `HintDetector::scan(&[String])` (`hints.rs:237`) fed from `grid()` | UNCHANGED; feed it `row_text` rows | None. hints.rs is engine-independent. |
| In-buffer search | UI `SearchState` over `grid()` (`:1462-1518`) | `row_text` for parity; OPTIONAL `RegexSearch` (unverified) to also search scrollback | Low. Current behavior is visible-grid only. |
| Command-history (Ctrl+R) | `history_store().fuzzy_search` (`history_search.rs:163`) | UNCHANGED (separate store, not emulator) | None. |
| Read visible lines for AI + thumbnails | `get_visible_lines(max)->Vec<String>` (`:1852`) | back with `row_text`; keep method signature | Low. Single chokepoint; AI panel does not yet consume it. |
| Send command / raw bytes | `send_command`/`send_bytes` (`:1748,:1772`) | UNCHANGED (pure PTY write) | None. |
| Broadcast to panes | UI key->bytes -> `send_bytes` per pane (`split_pane.rs:156-227`) | UNCHANGED; fan bytes to each pane's writer | None. De-dup key->bytes (`:1934` vs `split_pane.rs:170-203`) opportunistically. |
| Bell | `TerminalEvent::Bell` (`:384-386`); `trigger_visual_bell` (`:1722`) | engine `Event::Bell` via `EventListener` (unverified); WIRE it to `trigger_visual_bell` | Medium. Currently broken theater (`event_receiver` never read); rebuild must connect it. |
| Title / OSC 0;2 | `title()` (`:704`) + `TitleChanged` (`:395-408`) | engine `Event::Title(String)` (unverified); or keep cwd-polling titler | Low. Today titles come from cwd polling, not OSC; OSC path is dead. |
| Clipboard OSC 52 / color OSC 4/10/11 | declared `Clipboard`/`ColorChanged` variants (`:42-48`), never emitted | engine `ClipboardStore/Load`, `ColorRequest` (unverified) as NEW features | Low. Not functional today; optional add. |
| Sixel / Kitty graphics | `images.rs` parsers, ZERO consumers (dead) | NOT in alacritty; keep `images.rs` as sidecar overlay if ever wanted | Low. Dead today; no regression risk. |
| Session recording / playback | `Recording::add_output/input/resize` (`recording.rs:127`) | engine-independent byte tee; must add the missing I/O tap to make it work | Medium. Currently disconnected theater; tap PTY bytes if preserving. |
| Session/Project model | `Session{pty,terminal}` (`session.rs:74-202`) | update `Session.terminal` type, or drop unused live fields (live path bypasses Session) | Low. Secondary, largely-dead consumer. |

---

## 6. Verification harness (prove the fix on macOS)

The fix is proven by two independent gates: a headless grid-model assertion set (no display needed, runs in CI), and a live macOS render capture (the human-judged sample). Both must pass before deleting the old path (Phase 4). Run-before-you-judge: do not claim "fixed" from inspection.

### 6a. Torture-test input

A single file `tests/fixtures/torture.txt` exercising every failure mode, written so it is byte-stable:

- Mixed ASCII alignment ruler: ten lines of `0123456789` repeated to column 80, to expose drift (cause A) and ceil-pitch creep.
- Box-drawing block: a full `┌─┬─┐ │ ├─┼─┤ └─┴─┘` table; misalignment here is the canonical garble signature.
- CJK + emoji wide chars: `日本語テスト 中文测试 🐕🚀✅` interleaved with ASCII so a wide-char overrun visibly pushes the following ASCII (cause C).
- Combining marks / ZWJ: `é (e+U+0301)`, `👨‍👩‍👧‍👦` ZWJ family, `🇯🇵` flag (regional indicators).
- ANSI color: 16-color row, 256-cube ramp, 24-bit truecolor gradient line.
- Attributes: `bold`, `italic`, `underline`, `strikethrough`, `dim`, `inverse` each on its own labeled line.
- Last-column write + CR/LF to probe pending-wrap.
- A tab-stop line mixing `\t` with text.

### 6b. Headless grid-model unit tests (CI, no display)

In `crates/corgiterm-core` (and behind the `engine-alacritty` feature), assert on the engine's cell model, not on truthiness:

- `cargo test -p corgiterm-core` with new tests that feed byte sequences and assert: a CJK char produces a cell with `width==2` followed by a spacer cell; the cursor advances by 2 columns over a wide char; an SGR truecolor sequence yields the exact RGBA on the target cell; a combining sequence lands on one base cell (not two); resize from 80 to 40 cols reflows without dropping content. Each assertion checks the documented contract (the actual cell width / color / column), per no-theater: tests must fail when the model is wrong.
- Exact command: `cargo test --workspace` plus `cargo test -p corgiterm-core --features engine-alacritty -- --nocapture`.

### 6c. Live macOS render capture (human-judged sample)

- Build and run: `cargo build --release && RUST_LOG=corgiterm_ui=debug ./target/release/corgiterm`. The debug log must print the resolved monospace face name (confirms the Phase 0 font fix took on Core Text, since `fc-list` is the wrong probe on macOS, verified empty this session).
- Feed the torture file into the running terminal: `cat tests/fixtures/torture.txt` inside the app.
- Capture evidence with the verified-present `/usr/sbin/screencapture`: `screencapture -x -o /tmp/corgiterm_before.png` on the current (broken) build, then the same on the rebuilt build as `/tmp/corgiterm_after.png`. Human (the user) judges: box-drawing aligned, no CJK overrun, attributes/colors correct, no overlap/gaps on the ASCII ruler.
- Optional automated regression: a pixel-diff of the ASCII-ruler region across runs to detect drift creep, but the box-drawing/CJK judgment stays human in the loop for v1 (do not over-claim an automated pass/fail on glyph quality).
- Interactive parity (mandatory before claiming done): run `vim`, `less`, `tmux`, and a CJK-locale paste inside the rebuilt terminal to confirm PtyWrite reply path and reflow work end-to-end. This is the one thing a unit test cannot cover.

Honest scope: 6b proves the model is correct; 6c proves the macOS render is correct. Neither proves Alacritty-class crispness (not a goal of the model swap). At least one thing left unverified by design: GPU-renderer crispness and any Sixel/image rendering (engine drops graphics).

---

## 7. Open decisions for the user (confirm before execution)

1. **Engine pick.** Confirm alacritty_terminal 0.26 as the model (the recommendation), with the pango/cairo GTK4 renderer kept. If Sixel/Kitty image rendering or rich OSC-8 hyperlinks are a hard day-one requirement, say so now: that flips the choice to wezterm-term and accepts git-pinning + no crates.io publish. Default if you say nothing: alacritty_terminal 0.26.

2. **Renderer target: pango-done-right (v1) vs GPU atlas (later).** Confirm we ship the corrected Pango/Cairo grid-snap renderer first (fixes the garble, keeps scope bounded) and treat a GPU GLArea/glyphon renderer as a separate future project. If you want GPU crispness in this pass instead, that materially enlarges Phase 2 and changes the effort estimate.

3. **Cleanup scope (deletions + truth-in-UI).** Confirm we may, at Phase 4 after green verification: delete the dead `corgiterm-terminal` crate and the unused `wgpu`/`glyphon` deps (verified zero dependents), delete the dead `images.rs` (or keep it parked for a future Sixel sidecar), and fix the false "VTE Terminal Widget" text in the About dialog (`dialogs.rs:205,252`) since the app uses the vte parser crate, not the GNOME VTE widget. Also confirm whether to repair the two currently-broken theater features while we are in there (visual bell wiring, session recording I/O tap) or leave them out of scope.
